product: New Relic synthetic monitoring via NerdGraph

transport.rule: one GraphQL document per request; dynamic values travel in variables; the user API key never appears in URLs, documents, or exception text

discovery.single_match_rule: zero matches means create; exactly one match means update; more than one match (or a non-null nextCursor) is an ambiguity error
discovery.quoting_rule: monitor names containing a single quote or backslash are rejected locally before any request

mutations.update_rule: update addresses the monitor by entity guid; all monitor fields are optional on update

monitor_input.script_rule: the script is plain text (not base64) and travels byte-exact

secure_credentials.reference_syntax: $secure.KEY_NAME (dot notation only; keys are UPPERCASE alphanumeric/underscore)
secure_credentials.value_rule: stored values can never be read back through NerdGraph - only keys are visible - so a reconciler must pass $secure references through untouched and must never send, log, or synthesize credential values
secure_credentials.update_rule: when the desired state does not provide a script, the update omits the script field entirely so the stored script and its $secure references are preserved

rules: only keys present in the desired tag set are ever written; keys on the entity that the desired state does not mention are never touched
with a discovered entity, only keys whose current values differ from the desired values are included in the replace call
when current and desired tags already match, no tagging mutation is sent
when the caller supplies the guid directly (no discovery), all desired keys are replaced

limits.retry_rule: HTTP 429 is retried with doubling delays through the injected delay function; other non-2xx statuses surface immediately

field_error.description: Monitor period must be one of the documented enum values

fixtures.tag_error_message: Tag mutation limit exceeded for entity
